criticalRules: Scilab source macro uses simulationFunctionName="lsplit" with blockType="c" and near-zero geometry
Use SPLIT_f when one explicit data signal must fan out to multiple downstream blocks
Reference superblock examples show a default 3-output form; preserve the exact output count and port layout from the diagram variant you are mirroring
description: Data signal splitter / fan-out junction for explicit data links
anomalies: Near-zero width/height (0.3333333) makes it render like a junction point
Source macro defines one input and multiple outputs rather than a standard rectangular block body
name: SPLIT_f
commonUses: Forking one signal to multiple consumers
Breaking illegal direct fan-out before verification
Adding monitor/debug branches without changing the main signal path
